notes: Silicon2 should be scored as K-beauty portfolio distribution, not one viral brand. Stage 3 needs sell-through, reorder, inventory, receivables, OPM, and FCF.

company_name: D'Alba Global US channel / IPO rerating
notes: D'Alba has strong US channel visibility, but post-listing doubling means Stage 2 plus 4B-watch before sell-through and OPM proof.

company_name: CJ / Olive Young K-beauty retail platform option
notes: Olive Young is high-quality Stage 2 platform evidence, but CJ Stage 3 needs cash-flow, IPO, shareholder return, or NAV transmission.

company_name: Nongshim Shin Ramyun global staple brand
notes: Nongshim is the non-Samyang K-food staple reference. Stage 3 needs OP/EPS, ASP, reorder, and inventory stability.

company_name: Cosmax / Kolmar Korea / C&C International fast beauty supply chain
notes: K-beauty OEM/ODM can be earlier Stage 3 than single brands if customer diversification, repeat orders, OPM, and working capital are clean.
Dr.G validates Korean skincare strategic value but is reference evidence, not a listed-company Green signal without direct linkage.
Amorepacific proves K-beauty is not one bucket. China premium exposure can be hard 4C despite global K-beauty strength.
US export growth remains positive, but Stage 3 needs gross-margin buffer and price pass-through without demand loss.

company_name: F&F licensed brand / TaylorMade governance watch
notes: F&F-style license brands need sell-through, inventory, and M&A discipline; logo fatigue and China exposure keep it Watch/Red.
Consumer export Stage 3 requires sell-through and reorder, not shipment alone.
A single viral SKU can reach Stage 1/2 but cannot create Green without portfolio repeat demand and OP/EPS support.
